text.title: Frontend Component Practices Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Component file is 400 lines and renders 5 unrelated UI concerns.
antipattern.root-cause: Author kept appending features instead of extracting sub-components.
antipattern.fix: Split into single-responsibility children; lift shared state up; if data fetching is involved, separate into container vs presentational.

antipattern.symptom: Clickable <div> with onClick but no role, no keyboard handler; fails axe.
antipattern.root-cause: Used <div> for styling reasons; forgot keyboard + role attrs.
antipattern.fix: Use <button type='button'>. If layout requires div, add role='button', tabIndex=0, onKeyDown for Enter/Space.

antipattern.symptom: <Input value={x} defaultValue={y} onChange={...} /> displayed value drifts from prop x.
antipattern.root-cause: Component receives both value (controlled) and defaultValue (uncontrolled).
antipattern.fix: Pick one mode. Controlled: pass only value + onChange. Uncontrolled: pass only defaultValue + ref.

antipattern.symptom: Restyling Button changed the rendering of unrelated <button> elsewhere.
antipattern.root-cause: Component file had global selector button { ... } without CSS scoping.
antipattern.fix: Move styles into Button.module.css (or scoped <style>) and reference via styles.button.

antipattern.symptom: Visual regression slips through; component breaks on prop combination not covered by manual QA.
antipattern.fix: Add Component.test.tsx with Testing Library + Component.stories.tsx with at least 3 prop variants.
